# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

Security fixes are applied on a best-effort basis to the latest released version
of `lazyspotify` and the current `main` branch.

| Version | Supported |
| --- | --- |
| Latest release | Yes |
| `main` | Yes, best effort |
| Older releases | No |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

Please do not report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.

Use one of these private channels instead:

1. Prefer GitHub private vulnerability reporting:
   https://github.com/dubeyKartikay/lazyspotify/security/advisories/new
2. If that flow is unavailable, contact the maintainer privately through
   https://github.com/dubeyKartikay

Include as much of the following as possible:

- A description of the issue and its impact.
- Clear reproduction steps or a proof of concept.
- The affected version, commit, or installation method.
- Any suggested mitigation if you have one.

## Response Process

- We will acknowledge the report as soon as practical.
- We will investigate and validate the issue before discussing remediation.
- We may ask for additional details to reproduce or scope the vulnerability.
- Public disclosure should wait until a fix or mitigation is available.
